9 | 10 | // P3 — two decks + a crossfader + master volume: the actual mixer. 11 | // 12 | // Each deck builds its own stereo signal (deck.ts); App combines them here with an 13 | // equal-power crossfade (matching the desktop graph) and a master gain, then renders 14 | // the single output. One render effect owns the whole graph, so Elementary diffs the 15 | // combined tree on every state change and only nudges the consts that moved. 16 | 17 | export default function App() { 18 | const [audioReady, setAudioReady] = useState(false); 19 | const [error, setError] = useState(null); 20 | 21 | const deckA = useDeck('A', audioReady); 22 | const deckB = useDeck('B', audioReady); 23 | 24 | const [crossfader, setCrossfader] = useState(0); // -1 full A .. +1 full B 25 | const [masterVolume, setMasterVolume] = useState(0.8); 26 | 27 | // Boots the AudioContext + WebRenderer once, on the first deck's load gesture. 28 | const ensureAudio = useCallback(async () => { 29 | await initAudio(); 30 | setAudioReady(true); 31 | }, []); 32 | 33 | // The whole graph: (A·gainA + B·gainB)·master, per channel. 34 | useEffect(() => { 35 | if (!audioReady) return; 36 | const rt = getRuntime(); 37 | if (!rt) return; 38 | 39 | const silence = el.const({ value: 0 }); 40 | const a = buildDeckSignal(deckA.state); 41 | const b = buildDeckSignal(deckB.state); 42 | const aL = a?.left ?? silence; 43 | const aR = a?.right ?? silence; 44 | const bL = b?.left ?? silence; 45 | const bR = b?.right ?? silence; 46 | 47 | // Equal-power crossfade: at center both decks sit at ~0.707 (constant power). 48 | const t = (crossfader + 1) / 2; 49 | const gainA = el.const({ key: 'xfade_a', value: Math.cos(t * Math.PI * 0.5) }); 50 | const gainB = el.const({ key: 'xfade_b', value: Math.sin(t * Math.PI * 0.5) }); 51 | const master = el.const({ key: 'master_vol', value: masterVolume }); 52 | 53 | const outL = el.mul(el.add(el.mul(aL, gainA), el.mul(bL, gainB)), master); 54 | const outR = el.mul(el.add(el.mul(aR, gainA), el.mul(bR, gainB)), master); 55 | 56 | rt.core.render(outL, outR).catch((e) => setError(String(e))); 57 | }, [audioReady, deckA.state, deckB.state, crossfader, masterVolume]); 58 | 59 | return ( 60 |

This 5 | document is the plan we worked from: the goals, the scope decisions and their rationale, 6 | the architecture, the phase-by-phase delivery, and the notable technical decisions. 7 | 8 | It's meant to be readable on its own — by a student, a collaborator, or anyone deciding 9 | whether to build something similar. 10 | 11 | --- 12 | 13 | ## 1. Purpose 14 | 15 | Teach the **audio-graph and app-architecture ideas** behind a two-deck DJ mixer in the 16 | browser, without the native C++ engine, Electron, or hardware dependencies that make the 17 | desktop app hard to clone and run. 18 | 19 | Success looks like: a student clones one small repo, runs `npm install && npm run dev`, 20 | and has a working two-deck mixer they can read end to end in an afternoon. 21 | 22 | Non-goals: production fidelity, low-latency club use, format/codec completeness, or 23 | feature parity with the desktop app. 24 | 25 | --- 26 | 27 | ## 2. Scope decisions 28 | 29 | Each decision trades fidelity for clarity and reach. The right-hand column is *why*. 30 | 31 | | Area | Decision | Rationale | 32 | |------|----------|-----------| 33 | | **Tempo / pitch** | **Varispeed only** — tempo and pitch move together | Avoids a WASM time-stretch (WSOLA / signalsmith) rabbit hole; teaches mixing/EQ without the hardest DSP | 34 | | **Analysis** | **BPM only, in a Web Worker**; no key/phrase detection | One readable DSP pipeline off the main thread; key/phrase add cost without teaching much new | 35 | | **UI & state** | **Keep React + reducer-based state** | This is half the lesson; the desktop's component/state shape ports almost directly | 36 | | **MIDI** | **Dropped** | Hardware + Web MIDI is orthogonal to the core audio lesson | 37 | | **Persistence** | **None** — in-memory, session-only | No IndexedDB/SQLite; keeps the data layer to nearly nothing | 38 | | **Cue / headphone** | **In-mix cue point only**, no headphone bus | The browser realistically gives one stereo output; split monitoring needs multi-channel hardware | 39 | | **Waveform** | **Hand-drawn canvas**, not a library | Small, readable, and keeps the audio graph as the single source of truth for position | 40 | 41 | --- 42 | 43 | ## 3. Why this port is tractable (the key insight) 44 | 45 | The desktop app's split made this unusually clean: 46 | 47 | - The **entire mixer signal graph** (EQ, DJ filter, volume, metering, crossfader) was 48 | already written in **portable JavaScript** using `@elemaudio/core`. That code is 49 | renderer-agnostic. 50 | - The **native C++ addon** only added four things the graph *runs on or consumes*: 51 | 1. file decode (dr_libs), 52 | 2. offline analysis (BPM / key / phrase, FFT-heavy), 53 | 3. WSOLA time-stretching feeding the graph's inputs, and 54 | 4. PortAudio I/O plus the *native* Elementary runtime that executed the graph. 55 | 56 | Elementary already ships the browser target: **`@elemaudio/web-renderer`** runs the 57 | *same* `el.*` graph inside a Web Audio `AudioWorklet` compiled to WASM. So the port is 58 | not a rewrite — it's **replacing those four native responsibilities with browser 59 | equivalents** and changing how decks feed the graph. 60 | 61 | --- 62 | 63 | ## 4. Desktop → browser mapping 64 | 65 | | Desktop (native / Electron) | Browser-only replacement | Difficulty | 66 | |---|---|---| 67 | | Native Elementary runtime + PortAudio | `@elemaudio/web-renderer` → `AudioContext.destination` | Trivial | 68 | | Mixer graph (`el.*`) | Same code, `WebRenderer` instead of native `Renderer` | Trivial | 69 | | `dr_libs` decode | `AudioContext.decodeAudioData()` (WAV/MP3/FLAC) | Easy | 70 | | File dialog + fs | `` / drag-drop | Easy | 71 | | WSOLA time-stretch feeding `el.in()` | `el.table` reads of the decoded buffer, driven by a phasor/accum transport (varispeed) | Medium | 72 | | `getDeckPosition()` polling | `el.snapshot` on a metro → renderer event | Easy (reuses the meter event path) | 73 | | C++ BPM/key/phrase | BPM only, in a JS Web Worker (+ tap-tempo) | Medium | 74 | | `better-sqlite3` library | In-memory session list | Easy | 75 | | Hardware MIDI | — (dropped) | — | 76 | | Multi-channel cue/headphone | Single stereo output | (feature dropped) | 77 | 78 | --- 79 | 80 | ## 5. What we deliberately drop (the honest fidelity gaps) 81 | 82 | - **Real time-stretch** — varispeed changes pitch with tempo. True tempo-independent 83 | pitch would mean compiling `signalsmith-stretch` to WASM in the worklet. 84 | - **Key & phrase detection** — only BPM is estimated. 85 | - **Cue / split-headphone monitoring** — one stereo bus only. 86 | - **Output device selection, exclusive-mode / low latency** — browser defaults only. 87 | - **Beat-phase sync** — SYNC matches *tempo*, not beat alignment (see §9). 88 | 89 | --- 90 | 91 | ## 6. The deck audio model (the core idea) 92 | 93 | Each deck *is* Elementary nodes reading the track buffer directly. The transport phase 94 | is built from `el.accum`: 95 | 96 | ``` 97 | position = base + accum(increment, seekGen) 98 | ``` 99 | 100 | - **increment** — normalized progress per output sample: `0` when paused, 101 | `tempo / (frames − 1)` when playing. Scaling it by `tempo` *is* the varispeed. 102 | - **accum** — integrates the increment at **audio rate** (control-rate JS updates would 103 | zipper the audio). It resets to `0` whenever its reset input *changes by > 0.5*, so a 104 | monotonic `seekGen` counter triggers exactly one reset — that's a **seek**. 105 | - **base** — the normalized position of the last seek. 106 | 107 | Two consequences worth knowing: 108 | 109 | - **Stereo from a mono primitive.** `el.table` reads channel 0 only and maps `[0,1]` 110 | across the buffer, so a stereo track is stored as **two mono virtual-file-system (VFS) 111 | entries** (`A:L` / `A:R`) read with the *same* position signal. 112 | - **Looping** wraps the phase into `[loopIn, loopOut)` with a **floored modulo** 113 | (`x − len·floor(x/len)`), because `el.mod` is `fmod` and keeps the dividend's sign. 114 | Built structurally from a JS flag, so toggling the loop reshapes the graph while the 115 | accumulator keeps its state. Loop *exit* re-bases the transport in JS so playback 116 | continues from the current spot rather than the run-on phase. 117 | - **Playhead & meter** come back via `el.snapshot` (on a ~30 Hz metro) and `el.meter` 118 | events — the same event channel for both, kept in refs/low-rate state so they never 119 | trigger a graph re-render. 120 | 121 | --- 122 | 123 | ## 7. Phased delivery plan 124 | 125 | Each phase is a self-contained, runnable lesson. Status as of this writing: 126 | 127 | | Phase | Deliverable | Status | 128 | |------|-------------|--------| 129 | | **P0** | WASM audio boots; plays a test tone on a user gesture | ✅ | 130 | | **P1** | One deck: load → `decodeAudioData` → VFS → phasor/accum `el.table` playback; play/pause; click-to-seek; canvas waveform + snapshot playhead | ✅ | 131 | | **P2** | Per-deck signal chain: 3-band EQ + DJ LPF/HPF filter + volume + level meter (true stereo; control consts shared across L/R) | ✅ | 132 | | **P3** | Two decks + equal-power crossfader + master volume; one render effect owns the combined graph | ✅ | 133 | | **P4** | Varispeed tempo, manual loops (floored-mod wrap), in-mix cue point; cue/loop markers on the waveform | ✅ | 134 | | **P5** | BPM detection in a Web Worker (energy-onset → autocorrelation); beat-grid overlay; tap-tempo; tempo-match SYNC | ✅ | 135 | | **P6** | Session library panel + drag-to-deck (in-memory) | ⬜ planned | 136 | 137 | **Suggested teaching arc:** P0–P3 alone is a complete, satisfying two-deck mixer. P4–P5 138 | add the "DJ feel" (beatmatching, loops, cue). P6 is the app-shell/data-flow lesson. 139 | 140 | --- 141 | 142 | ## 8. Key technical decisions 143 | 144 | The non-obvious choices a future maintainer (or student) should understand: 145 | 146 | - **One render effect owns the whole graph.** `App` rebuilds the combined 147 | `(A·gainA + B·gainB)·master` graph on any control change; Elementary diffs against the 148 | previous graph, so unchanged nodes keep state and only moved consts update — no clicks. 149 | - **Equal-power crossfade** — `gainA = cos(t·π/2)`, `gainB = sin(t·π/2)`, 150 | `t = (x+1)/2`. Constant perceived loudness across the sweep (matches the desktop). 151 | - **High-frequency state stays out of the reducer.** Playhead position and meter level 152 | arrive ~20–30×/sec; they live in refs + light state, never in the graph-driving 153 | reducer. 154 | - **Cached waveform bitmap.** Peaks are rendered once to a normalized offscreen canvas; 155 | each frame is one `drawImage(visible slice)` + a playhead line. This (plus a 50 ms 156 | renderer event-poll interval) was the fix for an early jank→message-backlog→crash 157 | spiral. Lesson: don't redraw thousands of segments on every audio event. 158 | - **Amplitude-normalized waveform** so quiet tracks fill the height. 159 | - **Shared zoom.** A single `windowFrac` in `App` drives both waveforms, so zooming 160 | either scales both — which is what makes visual beat-alignment between decks possible. 161 | - **BPM pipeline** (`bpmWorker.ts`): short-time energy-flux onset envelope → 162 | autocorrelation (peak lag = period) → octave-fold into 80–160 BPM → beat-phase from the 163 | best-fitting pulse train. Simple and readable over accurate; **tap-tempo is the 164 | fallback** for material it misreads. 165 | - **Worker race-guard.** A `loadGen` counter discards a stale BPM result if another track 166 | loaded while analysis was running. 167 | 168 | --- 169 | 170 | ## 9. Known limitations & possible extensions 171 | 172 | - **AIFF import.** Works in Safari (`decodeAudioData` supports it); generally fails in 173 | Chrome/Firefox. A self-contained `decodeAiff.ts` fallback (parse FORM/COMM/SSND, 174 | big-endian PCM, 80-bit extended sample rate, linear resample to the context rate) would 175 | add cross-browser support with no new dependencies. Scoped to uncompressed PCM. 176 | - **Beat-phase sync.** SYNC currently matches effective tempo (`bpm·tempo`) only; nudging 177 | the playhead so downbeats align is the natural next increment. 178 | - **Real time-stretch.** Compile `signalsmith-stretch` (vendored in the desktop tree) to 179 | WASM in the worklet for tempo-independent pitch — the biggest single fidelity upgrade. 180 | - **Persistence.** Promote the session library to IndexedDB if it should survive reloads. 181 | 182 | --- 183 | 184 | ## 10. Tech stack & layout 185 | 186 | **Stack:** Vite · React 18 · TypeScript (strict) · `@elemaudio/core` + `@elemaudio/web-renderer` (4.x). No audio/DSP libraries beyond Elementary; no backend. 187 | 188 | ``` 189 | src/ 190 | audio.ts AudioContext + WebRenderer boot (50ms event poll) 191 | track.ts decode → VFS (L/R mono entries) + peaks + mono downmix 192 | deck.ts DeckState + buildDeckSignal (transport, EQ/filter/vol/meter, loop) 193 | useDeck.ts per-deck hook: reducer state, transport, playhead/meter, BPM, tap 194 | bpm.ts / bpmWorker.ts off-thread BPM + beat-phase estimation 195 | App.tsx composition: two decks, combined render graph, crossfader, sync, shared zoom 196 | components/ 197 | Waveform.tsx cached canvas: peaks + beat grid + cue/loop markers + zoom 198 | DeckPanel.tsx deck lane: load, waveform, transport, cue/loop/tap/sync 199 | DeckControls.tsx mixer strip: EQ + filter + tempo + volume/meter 200 | Knob.tsx / Fader.tsx controls 201 | Mixer.tsx crossfader + master (console center column) 202 | ``` 203 | 204 | **Run:** `npm install` then `npm run dev`; open the URL and click **Load track** (audio 205 | needs a user gesture). Build/typecheck: `npm run build`. src/App.tsx 1 | import { useCallback, useEffect, useState } from 'react'; 2 | import { el } from '@elemaudio/core'; 3 | import { initAudio, getRuntime } from './audio'; 4 | import { buildDeckSignal } from './deck'; 5 | import { useDeck } from './useDeck'; 6 | import DeckPanel from './components/DeckPanel'; 7 | import DeckControls from './components/DeckControls'; 8 | import Mixer from './components/Mixer'; 9 | 10 | // P3 — two decks + a crossfader + master volume: the actual mixer. 11 | // 12 | // Each deck builds its own stereo signal (deck.ts); App combines them here with an 13 | // equal-power crossfade (matching the desktop graph) and a master gain, then renders 14 | // the single output. One render effect owns the whole graph, so Elementary diffs the 15 | // combined tree on every state change and only nudges the consts that moved. 16 | 17 | export default function App() { 18 | const [audioReady, setAudioReady] = useState(false); 19 | const [error, setError] = useState(null); 20 | 21 | const deckA = useDeck('A', audioReady); 22 | const deckB = useDeck('B', audioReady); 23 | 24 | const [crossfader, setCrossfader] = useState(0); // -1 full A .. +1 full B 25 | const [masterVolume, setMasterVolume] = useState(0.8); 26 | 27 | // Boots the AudioContext + WebRenderer once, on the first deck's load gesture. 28 | const ensureAudio = useCallback(async () => { 29 | await initAudio(); 30 | setAudioReady(true); 31 | }, []); 32 | 33 | // The whole graph: (A·gainA + B·gainB)·master, per channel. 34 | useEffect(() => { 35 | if (!audioReady) return; 36 | const rt = getRuntime(); 37 | if (!rt) return; 38 | 39 | const silence = el.const({ value: 0 }); 40 | const a = buildDeckSignal(deckA.state); 41 | const b = buildDeckSignal(deckB.state); 42 | const aL = a?.left ?? silence; 43 | const aR = a?.right ?? silence; 44 | const bL = b?.left ?? silence; 45 | const bR = b?.right ?? silence; 46 | 47 | // Equal-power crossfade: at center both decks sit at ~0.707 (constant power). 48 | const t = (crossfader + 1) / 2; 49 | const gainA = el.const({ key: 'xfade_a', value: Math.cos(t * Math.PI * 0.5) }); 50 | const gainB = el.const({ key: 'xfade_b', value: Math.sin(t * Math.PI * 0.5) }); 51 | const master = el.const({ key: 'master_vol', value: masterVolume }); 52 | 53 | const outL = el.mul(el.add(el.mul(aL, gainA), el.mul(bL, gainB)), master); 54 | const outR = el.mul(el.add(el.mul(aR, gainA), el.mul(bR, gainB)), master); 55 | 56 | rt.core.render(outL, outR).catch((e) => setError(String(e))); 57 | }, [audioReady, deckA.state, deckB.state, crossfader, masterVolume]); 58 | 59 | return ( 60 |

Desktop DeckFlow fed already-stretched PCM 4 | // into the Elementary graph via el.in(); here the deck *is* Elementary nodes, reading 5 | // the track buffer directly with a phasor-style transport built from el.accum: 6 | // 7 | // position = base + accum(increment, seekGen) 8 | // 9 | // - increment : normalized progress per output sample. 0 when paused, tempo/(N-1) 10 | // when playing — so scaling it later gives varispeed (P4). 11 | // - accum : integrates increment at audio rate (control-rate JS updates would 12 | // zipper the audio). It resets to 0 whenever its reset input *changes* 13 | // by > 0.5, so a monotonic `seekGen` counter triggers exactly one reset. 14 | // - base : the normalized position we jumped to on the last seek. 15 | // 16 | // el.table maps position [0,1] across the whole buffer, reading channel 0 — so we read 17 | // the two mono VFS entries with the same position to get stereo. 18 | // 19 | // P2 adds the per-channel mixer chain — 3-band EQ, a DJ LPF/HPF filter, and volume — 20 | // ported from the desktop app's Elementary graph, plus a level meter. Control values 21 | // live on keyed const nodes shared across the L/R chains (Elementary dedups them), so 22 | // re-rendering only nudges a value; the filter/stateful nodes keep their state. 23 | 24 | import { el, type NodeRepr_t } from '@elemaudio/core'; 25 | import type { TrackData } from './track'; 26 | 27 | export interface DeckState { 28 | id: string; 29 | track: TrackData | null; 30 | playing: boolean; 31 | baseNorm: number; // normalized position of the last seek (0..1) 32 | seekGen: number; // bump to force the transport accumulator to reset 33 | tempo: number; // playback rate ratio; 1.0 in P1 (varispeed arrives in P4) 34 | volume: number; // 0..1 35 | eqLow: number; // dB, -12..12 36 | eqMid: number; // dB, -12..12 37 | eqHigh: number; // dB, -12..12 38 | filterCutoff: number; // -1 (LPF down to 100Hz) .. 0 (bypass) .. 1 (HPF up to 10kHz) 39 | cuePointNorm: number | null; // normalized cue point position 0..1, null if not set 40 | loopEnabled: boolean; // whether looping is active 41 | loopInNorm: number; // normalized loop in point 0..1 42 | loopOutNorm: number; // normalized loop out point 0..1 43 | } 44 | 45 | export function initialDeckState(id: string): DeckState { 46 | return { 47 | id, 48 | track: null, 49 | playing: false, 50 | baseNorm: 0, 51 | seekGen: 0, 52 | tempo: 1, 53 | volume: 1, 54 | eqLow: 0, 55 | eqMid: 0, 56 | eqHigh: 0, 57 | filterCutoff: 0, 58 | cuePointNorm: null, 59 | loopEnabled: false, 60 | loopInNorm: 0, 61 | loopOutNorm: 1, 62 | }; 63 | } 64 | 65 | export interface DeckSignal { 66 | left: NodeRepr_t; 67 | right: NodeRepr_t; 68 | } 69 | 70 | // Suffixes for this deck's analysis events, so the UI can route them by `source`. 71 | export const POS_EVENT_SUFFIX = '_pos'; 72 | export const METER_EVENT_SUFFIX = '_meter'; 73 | 74 | // DJ filter knob → cutoff frequencies. Center (0) bypasses (LPF 20kHz, HPF 20Hz); 75 | // left sweeps the LPF down to 100Hz, right sweeps the HPF up to 10kHz. 76 | export function filterCutoffs(cutoff: number): { lpf: number; hpf: number } { 77 | const lpf = cutoff < 0 ? 20000 * Math.pow(100 / 20000, Math.abs(cutoff)) : 20000; 78 | const hpf = cutoff > 0 ? 20 * Math.pow(10000 / 20, cutoff) : 20; 79 | return { lpf, hpf }; 80 | } 81 | 82 | // One channel's chain: 3-band EQ → DJ filter → volume. The keyed consts are shared 83 | // between the L and R chains (same key + value → one node), so a deck has one set of 84 | // controls driving both channels. 85 | function channelChain(sig: NodeRepr_t, s: DeckState): NodeRepr_t { 86 | const id = s.id; 87 | const fc = filterCutoffs(s.filterCutoff); 88 | 89 | let x = el.lowshelf( 90 | el.const({ value: 200 }), 91 | el.const({ value: 0.707 }), 92 | el.const({ key: `${id}_eqLow`, value: s.eqLow }), 93 | sig, 94 | ); 95 | x = el.peak( 96 | el.const({ value: 1000 }), 97 | el.const({ value: 0.707 }), 98 | el.const({ key: `${id}_eqMid`, value: s.eqMid }), 99 | x, 100 | ); 101 | x = el.highshelf( 102 | el.const({ value: 5000 }), 103 | el.const({ value: 0.707 }), 104 | el.const({ key: `${id}_eqHigh`, value: s.eqHigh }), 105 | x, 106 | ); 107 | // Smoothed cutoffs so knob moves don't zipper. 108 | x = el.lowpass( 109 | el.smooth(el.tau2pole(0.02), el.const({ key: `${id}_lpf`, value: fc.lpf })), 110 | el.const({ value: 1.2 }), 111 | x, 112 | ); 113 | x = el.highpass( 114 | el.smooth(el.tau2pole(0.02), el.const({ key: `${id}_hpf`, value: fc.hpf })), 115 | el.const({ value: 1.2 }), 116 | x, 117 | ); 118 | return el.mul(x, el.const({ key: `${id}_vol`, value: s.volume })); 119 | } 120 | 121 | /** 122 | * Builds the deck's stereo signal from its current state, or null if no track is 123 | * loaded. Const nodes carry stable `key`s so re-rendering only nudges their values 124 | * (no graph rebuild, no clicks). 125 | */ 126 | export function buildDeckSignal(s: DeckState): DeckSignal | null { 127 | if (!s.track) return null; 128 | 129 | const { pathL, pathR, totalFrames } = s.track; 130 | const incPerSample = s.tempo / Math.max(1, totalFrames - 1); 131 | 132 | const inc = el.const({ key: `${s.id}_inc`, value: s.playing ? incPerSample : 0 }); 133 | const seekTrig = el.const({ key: `${s.id}_seek`, value: s.seekGen }); 134 | const base = el.const({ key: `${s.id}_base`, value: s.baseNorm }); 135 | 136 | let position = el.add(base, el.accum(inc, seekTrig)); 137 | 138 | // Loop wrapping: when loop is enabled and valid, wrap position into [loopIn, loopOut) 139 | if (s.loopEnabled && s.loopInNorm < s.loopOutNorm) { 140 | const loopLen = s.loopOutNorm - s.loopInNorm; 141 | const loopStart = el.const({ key: `${s.id}_loopStart`, value: s.loopInNorm }); 142 | const loopLength = el.const({ key: `${s.id}_loopLen`, value: loopLen }); 143 | 144 | // Offset position to loop start 145 | const offset = el.sub(position, loopStart); 146 | 147 | // Floored modulo: x - len·floor(x/len) 148 | const wrapped = el.sub( 149 | offset, 150 | el.mul(loopLength, el.floor(el.div(offset, loopLength))) 151 | ); 152 | 153 | // Add back loop start 154 | position = el.add(loopStart, wrapped); 155 | } 156 | 157 | const leftRaw = el.table({ key: `${s.id}_tblL`, path: pathL }, position); 158 | const rightRaw = el.table({ key: `${s.id}_tblR`, path: pathR }, position); 159 | 160 | let left = channelChain(leftRaw, s); 161 | const right = channelChain(rightRaw, s); 162 | 163 | // Level meter on the post-fader left channel; passes audio through unchanged. 164 | left = el.meter({ key: `${s.id}_metertap`, name: `${s.id}${METER_EVENT_SUFFIX}` }, left); 165 | 166 | // Report the playhead position back to JS on a ~30Hz metro. snapshot outputs the 167 | // sampled value (not audio), so we fold it into the left channel multiplied by zero 168 | // — this keeps the node in the render tree without affecting what we hear. 169 | const posTap = el.snapshot( 170 | { key: `${s.id}_postap`, name: `${s.id}${POS_EVENT_SUFFIX}` }, 171 | el.metro({ key: `${s.id}_posmetro`, interval: 33 }), 172 | position, 173 | ); 174 | left = el.add(left, el.mul(el.const({ value: 0 }), posTap)); 175 | 176 | return { left, right }; 177 | } src/useDeck.ts 1 | // useDeck — React state + transport for a single deck. 2 | // 3 | // Owns the reducer-backed DeckState (the serializable transport + mixer controls) plus 4 | // two pieces of live, high-rate state that must NOT live in the reducer (they update 5 | // ~30x/sec and would otherwise trigger graph re-renders): the playhead position and 6 | // the meter level.  1 | // audio.ts — the single Web Audio + Elementary entry point.
 2 | //
 3 | // In the desktop DeckFlow, a native C++ runtime executed the Elementary graph and
 4 | // drove PortAudio. In the browser we use @elemaudio/web-renderer, which runs the
 5 | // exact same graph inside a WebAudio AudioWorklet compiled to WASM. The graph code
 6 | // (built with `el.*` from @elemaudio/core) is identical to the desktop app — only
 7 | // the *renderer* and the *output sink* change.
 8 | 
 9 | import WebRenderer from '@elemaudio/web-renderer';
10 | 
11 | export interface AudioRuntime {
12 |   ctx: AudioContext;
13 |   core: WebRenderer;
14 | }
15 | 
16 | let runtime: AudioRuntime | null = null;
17 | 
18 | /**
19 |  * Boots the AudioContext + Elementary WebRenderer. Must be called from a user
20 |  * gesture (click/tap) — browsers refuse to start an AudioContext otherwise.
21 |  * Safe to call repeatedly; it initializes once and resumes a suspended context.
22 |  */
23 | export async function initAudio(): Promise<AudioRuntime> {
24 |   if (runtime) {
25 |     if (runtime.ctx.state === 'suspended') await runtime.ctx.resume();
26 |     return runtime;
27 |   }
28 | 
29 |   const ctx = new AudioContext();
30 |   const core = new WebRenderer();
31 | 
32 |   // The renderer resolves to a WebAudio node that contains the WASM runtime.
33 |   // The third arg is the event-poll interval (ms): how often the worklet flushes
34 |   // meter/snapshot events to the main thread. 50ms (20Hz) is plenty for a playhead
35 |   // and meters, and keeps the worklet→main message rate low enough that a busy main
36 |   // thread can't let the queue back up.
37 |   const node = await core.initialize(
38 |     ctx,
39 |     {
40 |       numberOfInputs: 0,
41 |       numberOfOutputs: 1,
42 |       outputChannelCount: [2], // stereo out — the browser gives us one stereo bus
43 |     },
44 |     50,
45 |   );
46 | 
47 |   node.connect(ctx.destination);
48 | 
49 |   if (ctx.state === 'suspended') await ctx.resume();
50 | 
51 |   runtime = { ctx, core };
52 |   return runtime;
53 | }
54 | 
55 | export function getRuntime(): AudioRuntime | null {
56 |   return runtime;
57 | }
</content>

 1 | // track.ts — turn a user-selected audio file into something the audio graph can play.
 2 | //
 3 | // Desktop DeckFlow did this in native C++ (dr_libs decode → raw PCM, plus peak
 4 | // extraction). In the browser the platform hands us both for free:
 5 | //   - decodeAudioData() decodes WAV/MP3/FLAC and resamples to the AudioContext rate
 6 | //   - we downsample the PCM ourselves to draw a waveform
 7 | //
 8 | // One Elementary quirk shapes the data model: el.table reads *channel 0 only* of a
 9 | // buffer. So a stereo track becomes two mono virtual-file-system (VFS) entries — one
10 | // per channel — that the deck graph reads with a shared position signal.
11 | 
12 | import type { AudioRuntime } from './audio';
13 | 
14 | export interface TrackPeaks {
15 |   min: Float32Array;
16 |   max: Float32Array;
17 |   buckets: number;
18 | }
19 | 
20 | export interface TrackData {
21 |   name: string;
22 |   duration: number; // seconds
23 |   totalFrames: number; // frames at the AudioContext sample rate
24 |   sampleRate: number;
25 |   peaks: TrackPeaks;
26 |   pathL: string; // VFS key for the left channel
27 |   pathR: string; // VFS key for the right channel
28 | }
29 | 
30 | // Higher than the pixel width so a zoomed-in view still has detail to draw.
31 | const PEAK_BUCKETS = 6000;
32 | 
33 | function computePeaks(channel: Float32Array, buckets: number): TrackPeaks {
34 |   const min = new Float32Array(buckets);
35 |   const max = new Float32Array(buckets);
36 |   const bucketSize = channel.length / buckets;
37 | 
38 |   for (let b = 0; b < buckets; b++) {
39 |     const start = Math.floor(b * bucketSize);
40 |     const end = Math.min(channel.length, Math.floor((b + 1) * bucketSize));
41 |     let mn = 0;
42 |     let mx = 0;
43 |     for (let i = start; i < end; i++) {
44 |       const v = channel[i];
45 |       if (v < mn) mn = v;
46 |       if (v > mx) mx = v;
47 |     }
48 |     min[b] = mn;
49 |     max[b] = mx;
50 |   }
51 | 
52 |   return { min, max, buckets };
53 | }
54 | 
55 | /**
56 |  * Decodes `file`, loads its channels into the Elementary VFS under `${deckId}:L` /
57 |  * `${deckId}:R`, and returns the metadata + waveform peaks the UI needs.
58 |  */
59 | export async function loadTrackToVFS(
60 |   rt: AudioRuntime,
61 |   deckId: string,
62 |   file: File,
63 | ): Promise<TrackData> {
64 |   const arrayBuffer = await file.arrayBuffer();
65 |   // decodeAudioData resamples to rt.ctx.sampleRate, so frame counts below are already
66 |   // in the engine's sample rate — no resampling factor needed in the transport math.
67 |   const audioBuffer = await rt.ctx.decodeAudioData(arrayBuffer);
68 | 
69 |   const ch0 = audioBuffer.getChannelData(0);
70 |   const ch1 = audioBuffer.numberOfChannels > 1 ? audioBuffer.getChannelData(1) : ch0;
71 | 
72 |   // Copy into standalone Float32Arrays; getChannelData returns views onto the
73 |   // AudioBuffer that we don't want the VFS to alias.
74 |   const left = new Float32Array(ch0);
75 |   const right = new Float32Array(ch1);
76 | 
77 |   const pathL = `${deckId}:L`;
78 |   const pathR = `${deckId}:R`;
79 | 
80 |   // Must complete before any render references these paths, or the table node
81 |   // rejects the path as an invalid resource.
82 |   await rt.core.updateVirtualFileSystem({ [pathL]: left, [pathR]: right });
83 | 
84 |   return {
85 |     name: file.name,
86 |     duration: audioBuffer.duration,
87 |     totalFrames: audioBuffer.length,
88 |     sampleRate: audioBuffer.sampleRate,
89 |     peaks: computePeaks(ch0, PEAK_BUCKETS),
90 |     pathL,
91 |     pathR,
92 |   };
93 | }
</content>

  1 | // Waveform — canvas waveform with playhead, click-to-seek, and scroll-to-zoom.
  2 | //
  3 | // Performance matters here: the playhead moves ~20x/sec, so redrawing the full
  4 | // thousands-of-segments waveform every tick would jank the main thread (and, paired
  5 | // with the audio worklet's event stream, can spiral into a crash). Instead we render
  6 | // the peaks ONCE to an offscreen bitmap, normalized to fill the height, and each frame
  7 | // just blit the visible slice of that bitmap and stroke one playhead line.
  8 | //
  9 | // Zoom: the mouse wheel shrinks/grows the visible fraction of the track; when zoomed
 10 | // in, the view follows the playhead (centered, clamped at the ends) — like the
 11 | // desktop app's scrolling waveform.
 12 | 
 13 | import { useCallback, useEffect, useRef, useState } from 'react';
 14 | import type { TrackPeaks } from '../track';
 15 | 
 16 | interface Props {
 17 |   peaks: TrackPeaks | null;
 18 |   position: number; // normalized 0..1
 19 |   onSeek: (norm: number) => void;
 20 |   cuePoint: number | null; // normalized cue point position 0..1, null if not set
 21 |   loopIn: number | null; // normalized loop in point 0..1, null if not set
 22 |   loopOut: number | null; // normalized loop out point 0..1, null if not set
 23 |   loopEnabled: boolean; // whether looping is active
 24 | }
 25 | 
 26 | const CACHE_HEIGHT = 256; // offscreen bitmap height; scaled to the canvas at blit time
 27 | const MIN_WINDOW = 0.02; // closest zoom: 2% of the track visible
 28 | 
 29 | export default function Waveform({ peaks, position, onSeek, cuePoint, loopIn, loopOut, loopEnabled }: Props) {
 30 |   const canvasRef = useRef<HTMLCanvasElement>(null);
 31 |   const cacheRef = useRef<HTMLCanvasElement | null>(null);
 32 |   const [windowFrac, setWindowFrac] = useState(1); // fraction of track visible (1 = all)
 33 | 
 34 |   // Reset to the full-track view whenever a new track loads.
 35 |   useEffect(() => {
 36 |     setWindowFrac(1);
 37 |   }, [peaks]);
 38 | 
 39 |   // Render the peaks to an offscreen bitmap, normalized so the loudest peak fills the
 40 |   // height. Rebuilt only when the peaks change — not per frame.
 41 |   useEffect(() => {
 42 |     if (!peaks) {
 43 |       cacheRef.current = null;
 44 |       return;
 45 |     }
 46 |     const w = peaks.buckets;
 47 |     let off = cacheRef.current;
 48 |     if (!off) {
 49 |       off = document.createElement('canvas');
 50 |       cacheRef.current = off;
 51 |     }
 52 |     off.width = w;
 53 |     off.height = CACHE_HEIGHT;
 54 |     const c = off.getContext('2d');
 55 |     if (!c) return;
 56 | 
 57 |     let peak = 0;
 58 |     for (let i = 0; i < w; i++) {
 59 |       const m = Math.max(Math.abs(peaks.min[i]), Math.abs(peaks.max[i]));
 60 |       if (m > peak) peak = m;
 61 |     }
 62 |     const norm = peak > 0 ? 1 / peak : 1;
 63 |     const mid = CACHE_HEIGHT / 2;
 64 | 
 65 |     c.clearRect(0, 0, w, CACHE_HEIGHT);
 66 |     c.strokeStyle = '#4cc2ff';
 67 |     c.beginPath();
 68 |     for (let x = 0; x < w; x++) {
 69 |       c.moveTo(x + 0.5, mid - peaks.min[x] * norm * mid);
 70 |       c.lineTo(x + 0.5, mid - peaks.max[x] * norm * mid);
 71 |     }
 72 |     c.stroke();
 73 |   }, [peaks]);
 74 | 
 75 |   // The visible bucket window, centered on the playhead and clamped to the track.
 76 |   const windowFor = useCallback(
 77 |     (totalBuckets: number) => {
 78 |       const win = Math.max(1, Math.round(totalBuckets * windowFrac));
 79 |       const center = position * totalBuckets;
 80 |       const start = Math.max(0, Math.min(totalBuckets - win, center - win / 2));
 81 |       return { start, win };
 82 |     },
 83 |     [position, windowFrac],
 84 |   );
 85 | 
 86 |   const draw = useCallback(() => {
 87 |     const canvas = canvasRef.current;
 88 |     if (!canvas) return;
 89 |     const ctx = canvas.getContext('2d');
 90 |     if (!ctx) return;
 91 | 
 92 |     const dpr = window.devicePixelRatio || 1;
 93 |     const cssW = canvas.clientWidth;
 94 |     const cssH = canvas.clientHeight;
 95 |     if (cssW === 0 || cssH === 0) return;
 96 | 
 97 |     const bw = Math.round(cssW * dpr);
 98 |     const bh = Math.round(cssH * dpr);
 99 |     if (canvas.width !== bw || canvas.height !== bh) {
100 |       canvas.width = bw;
101 |       canvas.height = bh;
102 |     }
103 |     ctx.setTransform(dpr, 0, 0, dpr, 0, 0);
104 |     ctx.clearRect(0, 0, cssW, cssH);
105 | 
106 |     const cache = cacheRef.current;
107 |     if (!cache) {
108 |       ctx.strokeStyle = '#2b303d';
109 |       ctx.beginPath();
110 |       ctx.moveTo(0, cssH / 2);
111 |       ctx.lineTo(cssW, cssH / 2);
112 |       ctx.stroke();
113 |       return;
114 |     }
115 | 
116 |     const total = cache.width;
117 |     const { start, win } = windowFor(total);
118 | 
119 |     // Blit just the visible slice, scaled to the canvas.
120 |     ctx.drawImage(cache, start, 0, win, cache.height, 0, 0, cssW, cssH);
121 | 
122 |     // Draw loop region overlay (only if enabled)
123 |     if (loopEnabled && loopIn !== null && loopOut !== null) {
124 |       const loopInX = ((loopIn * total - start) / win) * cssW;
125 |       const loopOutX = ((loopOut * total - start) / win) * cssW;
126 |       
127 |       // Semi-transparent overlay
128 |       ctx.fillStyle = 'rgba(76, 194, 255, 0.2)';
129 |       ctx.fillRect(loopInX, 0, loopOutX - loopInX, cssH);
130 |     }
131 | 
132 |     // Draw loop in marker (always show if set)
133 |     if (loopIn !== null) {
134 |       const loopInX = ((loopIn * total - start) / win) * cssW;
135 |       
136 |       // Loop in marker (green)
137 |       ctx.strokeStyle = '#4ade80';
138 |       ctx.lineWidth = 2;
139 |       ctx.beginPath();
140 |       ctx.moveTo(loopInX, 0);
141 |       ctx.lineTo(loopInX, cssH);
142 |       ctx.stroke();
143 |       
144 |       // Add "IN" label
145 |       ctx.fillStyle = '#4ade80';
146 |       ctx.font = 'bold 12px sans-serif';
147 |       ctx.textAlign = 'left';
148 |       ctx.fillText('IN', loopInX + 4, 16);
149 |     }
150 |     
151 |     // Draw loop out marker (always show if set)
152 |     if (loopOut !== null) {
153 |       const loopOutX = ((loopOut * total - start) / win) * cssW;
154 |       
155 |       // Loop out marker (red)
156 |       ctx.strokeStyle = '#f87171';
157 |       ctx.lineWidth = 2;
158 |       ctx.beginPath();
159 |       ctx.moveTo(loopOutX, 0);
160 |       ctx.lineTo(loopOutX, cssH);
161 |       ctx.stroke();
162 |       
163 |       // Add "OUT" label
164 |       ctx.fillStyle = '#f87171';
165 |       ctx.font = 'bold 12px sans-serif';
166 |       ctx.textAlign = 'right';
167 |       ctx.fillText('OUT', loopOutX - 4, 16);
168 |     }
169 | 
170 |     // Draw cue point marker (yellow)
171 |     if (cuePoint !== null) {
172 |       const cueX = ((cuePoint * total - start) / win) * cssW;
173 |       ctx.strokeStyle = '#fbbf24';
174 |       ctx.lineWidth = 3;
175 |       ctx.beginPath();
176 |       ctx.moveTo(cueX, 0);
177 |       ctx.lineTo(cueX, cssH);
178 |       ctx.stroke();
179 |       
180 |       // Add triangle marker at top
181 |       ctx.fillStyle = '#fbbf24';
182 |       ctx.beginPath();
183 |       ctx.moveTo(cueX, 0);
184 |       ctx.lineTo(cueX - 5, 10);
185 |       ctx.lineTo(cueX + 5, 10);
186 |       ctx.closePath();
187 |       ctx.fill();
188 |       
189 |       // Add "CUE" label
190 |       ctx.font = 'bold 12px sans-serif';
191 |       ctx.textAlign = 'center';
192 |       ctx.fillText('CUE', cueX, 26);
193 |     }
194 | 
195 |     // Playhead at its true position within the visible window.
196 |     const playX = ((position * total - start) / win) * cssW;
197 |     ctx.strokeStyle = '#ff6b6b';
198 |     ctx.lineWidth = 2;
199 |     ctx.beginPath();
200 |     ctx.moveTo(playX, 0);
201 |     ctx.lineTo(playX, cssH);
202 |     ctx.stroke();
203 |   }, [position, windowFor, cuePoint, loopIn, loopOut, loopEnabled]);
204 | 
205 |   useEffect(() => {
206 |     draw();
207 |   }, [draw]);
208 | 
209 |   useEffect(() => {
210 |     const canvas = canvasRef.current;
211 |     if (!canvas) return;
212 |     const ro = new ResizeObserver(() => draw());
213 |     ro.observe(canvas);
214 |     return () => ro.disconnect();
215 |   }, [draw]);
216 | 
217 |   // Wheel zoom — attached natively so we can preventDefault (React's onWheel is passive).
218 |   useEffect(() => {
219 |     const canvas = canvasRef.current;
220 |     if (!canvas) return;
221 |     const onWheel = (e: WheelEvent) => {
222 |       e.preventDefault();
223 |       const factor = e.deltaY > 0 ? 1.2 : 1 / 1.2;
224 |       setWindowFrac((f) => Math.max(MIN_WINDOW, Math.min(1, f * factor)));
225 |     };
226 |     canvas.addEventListener('wheel', onWheel, { passive: false });
227 |     return () => canvas.removeEventListener('wheel', onWheel);
228 |   }, []);
229 | 
230 |   const handleClick = (e: React.MouseEvent<HTMLCanvasElement>) => {
231 |     const cache = cacheRef.current;
232 |     if (!cache) return;
233 |     const rect = e.currentTarget.getBoundingClientRect();
234 |     const frac = (e.clientX - rect.left) / rect.width;
235 |     const total = cache.width;
236 |     const { start, win } = windowFor(total);
237 |     onSeek((start + frac * win) / total);
238 |   };
239 | 
240 |   return <canvas ref={canvasRef} className="waveform" onClick={handleClick} />;
241 | }

Excellent! I've analyzed your DeckFlow Web application. Now let me grill you with some challenging questions about its architecture and implementation:

## 🔥 **Architecture & Design Questions**

**1. Transport Model Deep Dive:**
Your deck transport uses `position = base + accum(increment, seekGen)`. Why did you choose an **accumulator-based** transport at audio rate instead of controlling position from JavaScript? What would break if you tried to update position via React state at 60fps?

**2. Stereo Channel Strategy:**
You store stereo tracks as **two separate mono VFS entries** (`A:L` and `A:R`) instead of one stereo buffer. This seems inefficient - why is this necessary given Elementary's `el.table` limitation? What would happen if you tried to use a single stereo VFS entry?

**3. Loop Implementation:**
Your loop uses a **floored modulo** (`x - len·floor(x/len)`) instead of `el.mod`. Why? What's wrong with `fmod` for this use case, and what audio artifacts would you hear if you used it instead?

**4. Equal-Power Crossfade:**
You use `gainA = cos(t·π/2)` and `gainB = sin(t·π/2)`. Why equal-power instead of linear crossfading? What would the user perceive differently at the center position with a linear fade?

**5. High-Frequency State Isolation:**
Position and meter level live in **refs + useState**, never in the reducer. Why is this critical? What would happen to performance if you put `position` in the reducer that triggers the graph rebuild?

**6. Waveform Rendering Strategy:**
You render peaks **once to an offscreen canvas** then blit slices. The comment mentions this fixed a "jank→message-backlog→crash spiral." Explain this failure mode - what was the chain of events that led to a crash?

**7. Varispeed vs Time-Stretch:**
You chose **varispeed** (tempo and pitch linked) over real time-stretching. What DSP algorithm would you need to implement for independent pitch/tempo, and why is it "the hardest DSP" to add?

**8. BPM Detection Approach:**
Your BPM pipeline uses "energy-flux onset → autocorrelation → octave-fold into 80-160 BPM." Why autocorrelation specifically? What musical assumption does this make, and when would it fail catastrophically?

**9. Event Polling Rate:**
You poll renderer events at **50ms intervals** (20Hz). Why not faster for smoother playhead updates? What's the tradeoff you're managing here?

**10. Seek Generation Counter:**
`seekGen` is a monotonic counter that triggers transport resets when it changes by >0.5. Why use a counter instead of a boolean toggle? What edge case does this prevent?

---

## 🎯 **Implementation Challenges**

**11. Race Condition:**
The `loadGen` counter guards against stale BPM results. Walk me through the exact race condition this prevents. What would the user experience if you removed this guard?

**12. Graph Diffing:**
You say "Elementary diffs against the previous graph, so unchanged nodes keep state." How does this work internally? What happens to a `el.lowpass` filter's internal state when you change an unrelated EQ value?

**13. AudioContext Resume:**
Why must `initAudio()` be called from a **user gesture**? What happens if you try to create an AudioContext on page load? Is this a security feature or a performance optimization?

**14. Normalized Position:**
Everything uses normalized position (0..1) instead of frame counts. Why? What advantages does this give you, especially regarding sample rate conversions?

**15. Loop Exit Behavior:**
When toggling loop off, the comment says "position will naturally continue from where it was" but you don't update `baseNorm`. How does the transport know where to continue from? Trace through the math.

---

## 🚀 **Extension & Scaling Questions**

**16. Beat-Phase Sync:**
The spec mentions SYNC matches tempo but not beat alignment. How would you implement beat-phase sync? What additional data would you need to track, and where would you inject the phase correction?

**17. AIFF Support:**
You mention AIFF works in Safari but fails in Chrome. If you had to implement a fallback decoder, what are the three hardest parts of parsing AIFF (hint: the spec mentions them)?

**18. Multi-Output Routing:**
The browser gives you "one stereo bus." If you had access to a multi-channel audio interface, how would you route separate cue/headphone monitoring? What Web Audio API features would you use?

**19. Worklet Message Backpressure:**
What happens if the main thread gets blocked and can't consume meter/snapshot events fast enough? Does the AudioWorklet queue grow unbounded, or is there backpressure?

**20. Memory Management:**
When you load a new track, the old VFS entries (`A:L`, `A:R`) are replaced. Does Elementary automatically clean up the old buffers, or could you leak memory with repeated track loads?

---

## 💡 **Bonus Round: Show Me You Understand**

**21. The Key Insight:**
The spec says the port was "unusually clean" because the mixer graph was already in portable JavaScript. Explain why this architectural decision in the desktop app made the browser port trivial. What would have been different if the EQ/filters were in C++?

**22. Failure Mode:**
If a user loads a 2-hour DJ mix and sets tempo to 2.0x, what happens to the `increment` value? Could this cause numerical precision issues in the accumulator over time?

---

**Answer these questions to prove you understand not just *what* the code does, but *why* it's designed this way and what would break if you changed it.** 🎤

# 🔥 DeckFlow Web Architecture Quiz (with Answer Clues)

## 🎯 Architecture & Design Questions

**1. Transport Model Deep Dive:**
Your deck transport uses `position = base + accum(increment, seekGen)`. Why audio-rate accumulator instead of JS control?
- 📍 **Find answer in:** [`deck.ts:6-14`](src/deck.ts:6-14) - Read the comment about "control-rate JS updates would zipper the audio"
- 🔍 **Key concept:** What happens when you change audio parameters at 60fps vs 48kHz?

**2. Stereo Channel Strategy:**
Why store as two mono VFS entries (`A:L`, `A:R`) instead of one stereo buffer?
- 📍 **Find answer in:** [`deck.ts:16-17`](src/deck.ts:16-17) and [`track.ts:8-10`](src/track.ts:8-10)
- 🔍 **Key limitation:** What does `el.table` read from a buffer?

**3. Loop Implementation:**
Why use floored modulo (`x - len·floor(x/len)`) instead of `el.mod`?
- 📍 **Find answer in:** [`SPEC.md:112-115`](SPEC.md:112-115) - "because `el.mod` is `fmod` and keeps the dividend's sign"
- 🔍 **Key concept:** What's the difference between `fmod(-0.1, 1.0)` and floored modulo?

**4. Equal-Power Crossfade:**
Why `gainA = cos(t·π/2)` and `gainB = sin(t·π/2)` instead of linear?
- 📍 **Find answer in:** [`App.tsx:47-50`](src/App.tsx:47-50) and [`SPEC.md:149-150`](SPEC.md:149-150)
- 🔍 **Key concept:** What's "constant perceived loudness" and why does cos²+sin²=1 matter?

**5. High-Frequency State Isolation:**
Why do position/meter live in refs+useState, never in the reducer?
- 📍 **Find answer in:** [`useDeck.ts:3-6`](src/useDeck.ts:3-6) and [`SPEC.md:151-153`](SPEC.md:151-153)
- 🔍 **Key concept:** What happens if 30 updates/sec trigger graph re-renders?

**6. Waveform Rendering Strategy:**
Explain the "jank→message-backlog→crash spiral" that caching fixed.
- 📍 **Find answer in:** [`Waveform.tsx:3-7`](src/components/Waveform.tsx:3-7) and [`SPEC.md:154-158`](SPEC.md:154-158)
- 🔍 **Key concept:** Redrawing thousands of segments at 20Hz + audio events = ?

**7. Varispeed vs Time-Stretch:**
What DSP algorithm for independent pitch/tempo? Why is it "the hardest DSP"?
- 📍 **Find answer in:** [`SPEC.md:33`](SPEC.md:33), [`SPEC.md:82-83`](SPEC.md:82-83), and [`SPEC.md:177-179`](SPEC.md:177-179)
- 🔍 **Key terms:** WSOLA, signalsmith-stretch, WASM worklet

**8. BPM Detection Approach:**
Why autocorrelation? What assumption does it make? When does it fail?
- 📍 **Find answer in:** [`SPEC.md:162-166`](SPEC.md:162-166) - "peak lag = period"
- 🔍 **Key concept:** What musical structure does autocorrelation detect?

**9. Event Polling Rate:**
Why 50ms (20Hz) instead of faster for smoother playhead?
- 📍 **Find answer in:** [`audio.ts:33-36`](src/audio.ts:33-36) and [`SPEC.md:154-158`](SPEC.md:154-158)
- 🔍 **Key tradeoff:** Message rate vs main thread capacity

**10. Seek Generation Counter:**
Why monotonic counter instead of boolean toggle?
- 📍 **Find answer in:** [`deck.ts:11-13`](src/deck.ts:11-13) - "changes by > 0.5"
- 🔍 **Key concept:** What if you seek twice in rapid succession?

---

## 🎯 Implementation Challenges

**11. Race Condition:**
What exact race does `loadGen` prevent? What would user experience?
- 📍 **Find answer in:** [`SPEC.md:166-167`](SPEC.md:166-167)
- 🔍 **Scenario:** Load track A → analysis starts → load track B → A's BPM arrives

**12. Graph Diffing:**
How do unchanged nodes keep state when you change unrelated values?
- 📍 **Find answer in:** [`App.tsx:13-15`](src/App.tsx:13-15), [`deck.ts:21-22`](src/deck.ts:21-22), and [`deck.ts:123-124`](src/deck.ts:123-124)
- 🔍 **Key mechanism:** Stable `key` props on const nodes

**13. AudioContext Resume:**
Why require user gesture? Security or performance?
- 📍 **Find answer in:** [`audio.ts:18-21`](src/audio.ts:18-21)
- 🔍 **Browser policy:** Autoplay restrictions

**14. Normalized Position:**
Why 0..1 instead of frame counts? What advantage for sample rates?
- 📍 **Find answer in:** [`deck.ts:16-17`](src/deck.ts:16-17) and [`track.ts:65-66`](src/track.ts:65-66)
- 🔍 **Key insight:** `el.table` maps [0,1] across the buffer

- 📍 **Find answer in:** [`deck.ts:136-154`](src/deck.ts:136-154) and [`useDeck.ts:77-82`](src/useDeck.ts:77-82)
- 🔍 **Key insight:** Accumulator keeps integrating; position is already correct

- 📍 **Find answer in:** [`audio.ts:33-36`](src/audio.ts:33-36) and [`Waveform.tsx:3-7`](src/components/Waveform.tsx:3-7)

- 📍 **Find answer in:** [`track.ts:80-82`](src/track.ts:80-82) - `updateVirtualFileSystem` behavior

- 🔍 **Architecture:** Renderer-agnostic `el.*` code

- 📍 **Find answer in:** [`deck.ts:130`](src/deck.ts:130) - `incPerSample` calculation

<content># 🔥 DeckFlow Web Architecture Quiz

A comprehensive set of questions to test deep understanding of the DeckFlow Web application's architecture, implementation decisions, and design tradeoffs.

---

## 🎯 Architecture & Design Questions

### 1. Transport Model Deep Dive
**Question:** Your deck transport uses `position = base + accum(increment, seekGen)`. Why did you choose an audio-rate accumulator instead of controlling position from JavaScript? What would break if you tried to update position via React state at 60fps?

**Find answer in:** [`deck.ts:6-14`](src/deck.ts:6-14)  
**Key concept:** Read the comment about "control-rate JS updates would zipper the audio"  
**Hint:** What happens when you change audio parameters at 60fps vs 48kHz sample rate?

---

### 2. Stereo Channel Strategy
**Question:** You store stereo tracks as two separate mono VFS entries (`A:L` and `A:R`) instead of one stereo buffer. This seems inefficient - why is this necessary given Elementary's `el.table` limitation? What would happen if you tried to use a single stereo VFS entry?

**Find answer in:** [`deck.ts:16-17`](src/deck.ts:16-17) and [`track.ts:8-10`](src/track.ts:8-10)  
**Key limitation:** What does `el.table` read from a buffer?  
**Hint:** Check the comment about "el.table reads channel 0 only"

---

### 3. Loop Implementation
**Question:** Your loop uses a floored modulo (`x - len·floor(x/len)`) instead of `el.mod`. Why? What's wrong with `fmod` for this use case, and what audio artifacts would you hear if you used it instead?

**Find answer in:** [`SPEC.md:112-115`](SPEC.md:112-115)  
**Key concept:** "because `el.mod` is `fmod` and keeps the dividend's sign"  
**Hint:** What's the difference between `fmod(-0.1, 1.0)` and floored modulo?

---

### 4. Equal-Power Crossfade
**Question:** You use `gainA = cos(t·π/2)` and `gainB = sin(t·π/2)`. Why equal-power instead of linear crossfading? What would the user perceive differently at the center position with a linear fade?

**Find answer in:** [`App.tsx:47-50`](src/App.tsx:47-50) and [`SPEC.md:149-150`](SPEC.md:149-150)  
**Key concept:** What's "constant perceived loudness" and why does cos²+sin²=1 matter?  
**Hint:** At center position, both decks should be at ~0.707 gain

---

### 5. High-Frequency State Isolation
**Question:** Position and meter level live in refs + useState, never in the reducer. Why is this critical? What would happen to performance if you put `position` in the reducer that triggers the graph rebuild?

**Find answer in:** [`useDeck.ts:3-6`](src/useDeck.ts:3-6) and [`SPEC.md:151-153`](SPEC.md:151-153)  
**Key concept:** What happens if 30 updates/sec trigger graph re-renders?  
**Hint:** "High-frequency state stays out of the reducer"

---

### 6. Waveform Rendering Strategy
**Question:** You render peaks once to an offscreen canvas then blit slices. The comment mentions this fixed a "jank→message-backlog→crash spiral." Explain this failure mode - what was the chain of events that led to a crash?

**Find answer in:** [`Waveform.tsx:3-7`](src/components/Waveform.tsx:3-7) and [`SPEC.md:154-158`](SPEC.md:154-158)  
**Key concept:** Redrawing thousands of segments at 20Hz + audio events = ?  
**Hint:** "Cached waveform bitmap" and "50 ms renderer event-poll interval"

---

### 7. Varispeed vs Time-Stretch
**Question:** You chose varispeed (tempo and pitch linked) over real time-stretching. What DSP algorithm would you need to implement for independent pitch/tempo, and why is it "the hardest DSP" to add?

**Find answer in:** [`SPEC.md:33`](SPEC.md:33), [`SPEC.md:82-83`](SPEC.md:82-83), and [`SPEC.md:177-179`](SPEC.md:177-179)  
**Key terms:** WSOLA, signalsmith-stretch, WASM worklet  
**Hint:** "Avoids a WASM time-stretch (WSOLA / signalsmith) rabbit hole"

---

### 8. BPM Detection Approach
**Question:** Your BPM pipeline uses "energy-flux onset → autocorrelation → octave-fold into 80-160 BPM." Why autocorrelation specifically? What musical assumption does this make, and when would it fail catastrophically?

**Find answer in:** [`SPEC.md:162-166`](SPEC.md:162-166)  
**Key concept:** "peak lag = period"  
**Hint:** What musical structure does autocorrelation detect? What about non-periodic music?

---

### 9. Event Polling Rate
**Question:** You poll renderer events at 50ms intervals (20Hz). Why not faster for smoother playhead updates? What's the tradeoff you're managing here?

**Find answer in:** [`audio.ts:33-36`](src/audio.ts:33-36) and [`SPEC.md:154-158`](SPEC.md:154-158)  
**Key tradeoff:** Message rate vs main thread capacity  
**Hint:** "keeps the worklet→main message rate low enough that a busy main thread can't let the queue back up"

---

### 10. Seek Generation Counter
**Question:** `seekGen` is a monotonic counter that triggers transport resets when it changes by >0.5. Why use a counter instead of a boolean toggle? What edge case does this prevent?

**Find answer in:** [`deck.ts:11-13`](src/deck.ts:11-13)  
**Key concept:** "changes by > 0.5"  
**Hint:** What if you seek twice in rapid succession with a boolean?

---

## 🎯 Implementation Challenges

### 11. Race Condition
**Question:** The `loadGen` counter guards against stale BPM results. Walk me through the exact race condition this prevents. What would the user experience if you removed this guard?

**Find answer in:** [`SPEC.md:166-167`](SPEC.md:166-167)  
**Scenario:** Load track A → analysis starts → load track B → A's BPM arrives  
**Hint:** "Worker race-guard"

---

### 12. Graph Diffing
**Question:** You say "Elementary diffs against the previous graph, so unchanged nodes keep state." How does this work internally? What happens to a `el.lowpass` filter's internal state when you change an unrelated EQ value?

**Find answer in:** [`App.tsx:13-15`](src/App.tsx:13-15), [`deck.ts:21-22`](src/deck.ts:21-22), and [`deck.ts:123-124`](src/deck.ts:123-124)  
**Key mechanism:** Stable `key` props on const nodes  
**Hint:** "Const nodes carry stable keys so re-rendering only nudges their values"

---

### 13. AudioContext Resume
**Question:** Why must `initAudio()` be called from a user gesture? What happens if you try to create an AudioContext on page load? Is this a security feature or a performance optimization?

**Find answer in:** [`audio.ts:18-21`](src/audio.ts:18-21)  
**Browser policy:** Autoplay restrictions  
**Hint:** "Must be called from a user gesture (click/tap) — browsers refuse to start an AudioContext otherwise"

---

### 14. Normalized Position
**Question:** Everything uses normalized position (0..1) instead of frame counts. Why? What advantages does this give you, especially regarding sample rate conversions?

**Find answer in:** [`deck.ts:16-17`](src/deck.ts:16-17) and [`track.ts:65-66`](src/track.ts:65-66)  
**Key insight:** `el.table` maps [0,1] across the buffer  
**Hint:** "decodeAudioData resamples to rt.ctx.sampleRate"

---

**Question:** When toggling loop off, the comment says "position will naturally continue from where it was" but you don't update `baseNorm`. How does the transport know where to continue from? Trace through the math.

**Find answer in:** [`deck.ts:136-154`](src/deck.ts:136-154) and [`useDeck.ts:77-82`](src/useDeck.ts:77-82)  
**Key insight:** Accumulator keeps integrating; position is already correct  
**Hint:** The accumulator state persists even when the loop wrapping is removed from the graph

---

## 🚀 Extension & Scaling Questions

### 16. Beat-Phase Sync
**Question:** The spec mentions SYNC matches tempo but not beat alignment. How would you implement beat-phase sync? What additional data would you need to track, and where would you inject the phase correction?

**Find answer in:** [`SPEC.md:87-88`](SPEC.md:87-88) and [`SPEC.md:175-177`](SPEC.md:175-177)  
**Hint:** BPM pipeline already computes "beat-phase from best-fitting pulse train"  
**Key question:** How do you nudge the playhead to align downbeats?

---

### 17. AIFF Support
**Question:** You mention AIFF works in Safari but fails in Chrome. If you had to implement a fallback decoder, what are the three hardest parts of parsing AIFF?

**Find answer in:** [`SPEC.md:172-175`](SPEC.md:172-175)  
**Key challenges:** 
1. FORM/COMM/SSND parsing
2. Big-endian PCM
3. 80-bit extended sample rate

---

### 18. Multi-Output Routing
**Question:** The browser gives you "one stereo bus." If you had access to a multi-channel audio interface, how would you route separate cue/headphone monitoring? What Web Audio API features would you use?

**Find answer in:** [`SPEC.md:38`](SPEC.md:38) and [`SPEC.md:76`](SPEC.md:76)  
**Web Audio API:** `AudioContext.destination` channels, routing graph  
**Hint:** "split monitoring needs multi-channel hardware"

---

### 19. Worklet Message Backpressure
**Question:** What happens if the main thread gets blocked and can't consume meter/snapshot events fast enough? Does the AudioWorklet queue grow unbounded, or is there backpressure?

**Find answer in:** [`audio.ts:33-36`](src/audio.ts:33-36) and [`Waveform.tsx:3-7`](src/components/Waveform.tsx:3-7)  
**Key insight:** Why 50ms interval was chosen  
**Hint:** "a busy main thread can't let the queue back up"

---

### 20. Memory Management
**Question:** When you load a new track, the old VFS entries (`A:L`, `A:R`) are replaced. Does Elementary automatically clean up the old buffers, or could you leak memory with repeated track loads?

**Find answer in:** [`track.ts:80-82`](src/track.ts:80-82)  
**Key question:** Does `updateVirtualFileSystem` replace or append?  
**Hint:** Look at how the VFS keys are structured with deck IDs

---

## 💡 Bonus Round: Show Me You Understand

### 21. The Key Insight
**Question:** The spec says the port was "unusually clean" because the mixer graph was already in portable JavaScript. Explain why this architectural decision in the desktop app made the browser port trivial. What would have been different if the EQ/filters were in C++?

**Find answer in:** [`SPEC.md:43-59`](SPEC.md:43-59) - "Why this port is tractable (the key insight)"  
**Architecture:** Renderer-agnostic `el.*` code  
**Key point:** "The entire mixer signal graph was already written in portable JavaScript"

---

### 22. Failure Mode: Numerical Precision
**Question:** If a user loads a 2-hour DJ mix and sets tempo to 2.0x, what happens to the `increment` value? Could this cause numerical precision issues in the accumulator over time?

**Find answer in:** [`deck.ts:130`](src/deck.ts:130)  
**Math challenge:** 
- 2 hours = 7,200 seconds
- At 48kHz = 345,600,000 samples
- At 2.0x tempo = 691,200,000 accumulations
- Float32 precision = ~7 significant digits

**Key question:** Does the accumulator drift? What's the actual precision loss?

---

## 📚 Study Guide

### Essential Files to Master
1. **[`deck.ts`](src/deck.ts)** - Core audio graph and transport model
2. **[`useDeck.ts`](src/useDeck.ts)** - State management and event handling
3. **[`audio.ts`](src/audio.ts)** - Web Audio + Elementary initialization
4. **[`track.ts`](src/track.ts)** - File loading and VFS management
5. **[`App.tsx`](src/App.tsx)** - Graph composition and crossfading
6. **[`Waveform.tsx`](src/components/Waveform.tsx)** - Performance-critical rendering
7. **[`SPEC.md`](SPEC.md)** - Complete architectural documentation

### Key Concepts to Understand
- **Audio-rate vs control-rate** signal processing
- **Elementary's graph diffing** and node keying
- **VFS (Virtual File System)** in Elementary
- **Equal-power crossfading** mathematics
- **Accumulator-based transport** model
- **Event polling** and backpressure management
- **Canvas rendering optimization** techniques
- **React state management** for high-frequency updates

### Advanced Topics
- **DSP algorithms:** WSOLA, time-stretching, autocorrelation
- **Web Audio API:** AudioContext, AudioWorklet, WASM
- **Browser constraints:** Autoplay policies, single output bus
- **Performance optimization:** Offscreen canvas, event throttling
- **Race conditions:** Async loading, worker communication

---

## 🎓 How to Use This Quiz

1. **Self-Study:** Try answering each question before looking at the hints
2. **Code Review:** Use the file references to verify your understanding
3. **Deep Dive:** Follow the hint chains to understand the full context
4. **Discussion:** Use these questions to explain the architecture to others
5. **Interview Prep:** These cover real architectural decisions and tradeoffs

**Remember:** The goal isn't just to know *what* the code does, but *why* it's designed this way and what would break if you changed it.

<content lines="1-177">
  1 | // deck.ts — the deck's audio graph and state shape.
  2 | //
  3 | // This is the heart of the browser port. Desktop DeckFlow fed already-stretched PCM
  4 | // into the Elementary graph via el.in(); here the deck *is* Elementary nodes, reading
  5 | // the track buffer directly with a phasor-style transport built from el.accum:
  6 | //
  7 | //     position = base + accum(increment, seekGen)
  8 | //
  9 | //   - increment : normalized progress per output sample. 0 when paused, tempo/(N-1)
 10 | //                 when playing — so scaling it later gives varispeed (P4).
 11 | //   - accum     : integrates increment at audio rate (control-rate JS updates would
 12 | //                 zipper the audio). It resets to 0 whenever its reset input *changes*
 13 | //                 by > 0.5, so a monotonic `seekGen` counter triggers exactly one reset.
 14 | //   - base      : the normalized position we jumped to on the last seek.
 15 | //
 16 | // el.table maps position [0,1] across the whole buffer, reading channel 0 — so we read
 17 | // the two mono VFS entries with the same position to get stereo.
 18 | //
 19 | // P2 adds the per-channel mixer chain — 3-band EQ, a DJ LPF/HPF filter, and volume —
 20 | // ported from the desktop app's Elementary graph, plus a level meter. Control values
 21 | // live on keyed const nodes shared across the L/R chains (Elementary dedups them), so
 22 | // re-rendering only nudges a value; the filter/stateful nodes keep their state.
 23 | 
 24 | import { el, type NodeRepr_t } from '@elemaudio/core';
 25 | import type { TrackData } from './track';
 26 | 
 27 | export interface DeckState {
 28 |   id: string;
 29 |   track: TrackData | null;
 30 |   playing: boolean;
 31 |   baseNorm: number; // normalized position of the last seek (0..1)
 32 |   seekGen: number; // bump to force the transport accumulator to reset
 33 |   tempo: number; // playback rate ratio; 1.0 in P1 (varispeed arrives in P4)
 34 |   volume: number; // 0..1
 35 |   eqLow: number; // dB, -12..12
 36 |   eqMid: number; // dB, -12..12
 37 |   eqHigh: number; // dB, -12..12
 38 |   filterCutoff: number; // -1 (LPF down to 100Hz) .. 0 (bypass) .. 1 (HPF up to 10kHz)
 39 |   cuePointNorm: number | null; // normalized cue point position 0..1, null if not set
 40 |   loopEnabled: boolean; // whether looping is active
 41 |   loopInNorm: number; // normalized loop in point 0..1
 42 |   loopOutNorm: number; // normalized loop out point 0..1
 43 | }
 44 | 
 45 | export function initialDeckState(id: string): DeckState {
 46 |   return {
 47 |     id,
 48 |     track: null,
 49 |     playing: false,
 50 |     baseNorm: 0,
 51 |     seekGen: 0,
 52 |     tempo: 1,
 53 |     volume: 1,
 54 |     eqLow: 0,
 55 |     eqMid: 0,
 56 |     eqHigh: 0,
 57 |     filterCutoff: 0,
 58 |     cuePointNorm: null,
 59 |     loopEnabled: false,
 60 |     loopInNorm: 0,
 61 |     loopOutNorm: 1,
 62 |   };
 63 | }
 64 | 
 65 | export interface DeckSignal {
 66 |   left: NodeRepr_t;
 67 |   right: NodeRepr_t;
 68 | }
 69 | 
 70 | // Suffixes for this deck's analysis events, so the UI can route them by `source`.
 71 | export const POS_EVENT_SUFFIX = '_pos';
 72 | export const METER_EVENT_SUFFIX = '_meter';
 73 | 
 74 | // DJ filter knob → cutoff frequencies. Center (0) bypasses (LPF 20kHz, HPF 20Hz);
 75 | // left sweeps the LPF down to 100Hz, right sweeps the HPF up to 10kHz.
 76 | export function filterCutoffs(cutoff: number): { lpf: number; hpf: number } {
 77 |   const lpf = cutoff < 0 ? 20000 * Math.pow(100 / 20000, Math.abs(cutoff)) : 20000;
 78 |   const hpf = cutoff > 0 ? 20 * Math.pow(10000 / 20, cutoff) : 20;
 79 |   return { lpf, hpf };
 80 | }
 81 | 
 82 | // One channel's chain: 3-band EQ → DJ filter → volume. The keyed consts are shared
 83 | // between the L and R chains (same key + value → one node), so a deck has one set of
 84 | // controls driving both channels.
 85 | function channelChain(sig: NodeRepr_t, s: DeckState): NodeRepr_t {
 86 |   const id = s.id;
 87 |   const fc = filterCutoffs(s.filterCutoff);
 88 | 
 89 |   let x = el.lowshelf(
 90 |     el.const({ value: 200 }),
 91 |     el.const({ value: 0.707 }),
 92 |     el.const({ key: `${id}_eqLow`, value: s.eqLow }),
 93 |     sig,
 94 |   );
 95 |   x = el.peak(
 96 |     el.const({ value: 1000 }),
 97 |     el.const({ value: 0.707 }),
 98 |     el.const({ key: `${id}_eqMid`, value: s.eqMid }),
 99 |     x,
100 |   );
101 |   x = el.highshelf(
102 |     el.const({ value: 5000 }),
103 |     el.const({ value: 0.707 }),
104 |     el.const({ key: `${id}_eqHigh`, value: s.eqHigh }),
105 |     x,
106 |   );
107 |   // Smoothed cutoffs so knob moves don't zipper.
108 |   x = el.lowpass(
109 |     el.smooth(el.tau2pole(0.02), el.const({ key: `${id}_lpf`, value: fc.lpf })),
110 |     el.const({ value: 1.2 }),
111 |     x,
112 |   );
113 |   x = el.highpass(
114 |     el.smooth(el.tau2pole(0.02), el.const({ key: `${id}_hpf`, value: fc.hpf })),
115 |     el.const({ value: 1.2 }),
116 |     x,
117 |   );
118 |   return el.mul(x, el.const({ key: `${id}_vol`, value: s.volume }));
119 | }
120 | 
121 | /**
122 |  * Builds the deck's stereo signal from its current state, or null if no track is
123 |  * loaded. Const nodes carry stable `key`s so re-rendering only nudges their values
124 |  * (no graph rebuild, no clicks).
125 |  */
126 | export function buildDeckSignal(s: DeckState): DeckSignal | null {
127 |   if (!s.track) return null;
128 | 
129 |   const { pathL, pathR, totalFrames } = s.track;
130 |   const incPerSample = s.tempo / Math.max(1, totalFrames - 1);
131 | 
132 |   const inc = el.const({ key: `${s.id}_inc`, value: s.playing ? incPerSample : 0 });
133 |   const seekTrig = el.const({ key: `${s.id}_seek`, value: s.seekGen });
134 |   const base = el.const({ key: `${s.id}_base`, value: s.baseNorm });
135 | 
136 |   let position = el.add(base, el.accum(inc, seekTrig));
137 | 
138 |   // Loop wrapping: when loop is enabled and valid, wrap position into [loopIn, loopOut)
139 |   if (s.loopEnabled && s.loopInNorm < s.loopOutNorm) {
140 |     const loopLen = s.loopOutNorm - s.loopInNorm;
141 |     const loopStart = el.const({ key: `${s.id}_loopStart`, value: s.loopInNorm });
142 |     const loopLength = el.const({ key: `${s.id}_loopLen`, value: loopLen });
143 |     
144 |     // Offset position to loop start
145 |     const offset = el.sub(position, loopStart);
146 |     
147 |     // Floored modulo: x - len·floor(x/len)
148 |     const wrapped = el.sub(
149 |       offset,
150 |       el.mul(loopLength, el.floor(el.div(offset, loopLength)))
151 |     );
152 |     
153 |     // Add back loop start
154 |     position = el.add(loopStart, wrapped);
155 |   }
156 | 
157 |   const leftRaw = el.table({ key: `${s.id}_tblL`, path: pathL }, position);
158 |   const rightRaw = el.table({ key: `${s.id}_tblR`, path: pathR }, position);
159 | 
160 |   let left = channelChain(leftRaw, s);
161 |   const right = channelChain(rightRaw, s);
162 | 
163 |   // Level meter on the post-fader left channel; passes audio through unchanged.
164 |   left = el.meter({ key: `${s.id}_metertap`, name: `${s.id}${METER_EVENT_SUFFIX}` }, left);
165 | 
166 |   // Report the playhead position back to JS on a ~30Hz metro. snapshot outputs the
167 |   // sampled value (not audio), so we fold it into the left channel multiplied by zero
168 |   // — this keeps the node in the render tree without affecting what we hear.
169 |   const posTap = el.snapshot(
170 |     { key: `${s.id}_postap`, name: `${s.id}${POS_EVENT_SUFFIX}` },
171 |     el.metro({ key: `${s.id}_posmetro`, interval: 33 }),
172 |     position,
173 |   );
174 |   left = el.add(left, el.mul(el.const({ value: 0 }), posTap));
175 | 
176 |   return { left, right };
177 | }
</content>

<file><path>src/useDeck.ts</path>
<content lines="1-185">
  1 | // useDeck — React state + transport for a single deck.
  2 | //
  3 | // Owns the reducer-backed DeckState (the serializable transport + mixer controls) plus
  4 | // two pieces of live, high-rate state that must NOT live in the reducer (they update
  5 | // ~30x/sec and would otherwise trigger graph re-renders): the playhead position and
  6 | // the meter level. Both arrive asynchronously from the audio graph's analysis events.
  7 | 
  8 | import { useCallback, useEffect, useReducer, useRef, useState } from 'react';
  9 | import { getRuntime } from './audio';
 10 | import { loadTrackToVFS } from './track';
 11 | import {
 12 |   DeckState,
 13 |   initialDeckState,
 14 |   METER_EVENT_SUFFIX,
 15 |   POS_EVENT_SUFFIX,
 16 | } from './deck';
 17 | 
 18 | type EqBand = 'eqLow' | 'eqMid' | 'eqHigh';
 19 | 
 20 | type Action =
 21 |   | { type: 'LOAD'; track: DeckState['track'] }
 22 |   | { type: 'PLAY' }
 23 |   | { type: 'PAUSE' }
 24 |   | { type: 'SEEK'; norm: number }
 25 |   | { type: 'END' }
 26 |   | { type: 'SET_VOLUME'; value: number }
 27 |   | { type: 'SET_EQ'; band: EqBand; value: number }
 28 |   | { type: 'SET_FILTER'; value: number }
 29 |   | { type: 'SET_TEMPO'; value: number }
 30 |   | { type: 'SET_CUE'; norm: number }
 31 |   | { type: 'GO_TO_CUE' }
 32 |   | { type: 'CLEAR_CUE' }
 33 |   | { type: 'SET_LOOP_IN'; norm: number }
 34 |   | { type: 'SET_LOOP_OUT'; norm: number }
 35 |   | { type: 'TOGGLE_LOOP' }
 36 |   | { type: 'CLEAR_LOOP' };
 37 | 
 38 | const clamp01 = (n: number) => Math.min(1, Math.max(0, n));
 39 | const clampTempo = (n: number) => Math.min(2, Math.max(0.5, n));
 40 | 
 41 | function reducer(s: DeckState, a: Action): DeckState {
 42 |   switch (a.type) {
 43 |     case 'LOAD':
 44 |       // New track: stop, rewind, clear cue, and bump seekGen so the transport accumulator resets.
 45 |       return { ...s, track: a.track, playing: false, baseNorm: 0, seekGen: s.seekGen + 1, tempo: 1, cuePointNorm: null };
 46 |     case 'PLAY':
 47 |       return s.track ? { ...s, playing: true } : s;
 48 |     case 'PAUSE':
 49 |       return { ...s, playing: false };
 50 |     case 'SEEK':
 51 |       return s.track ? { ...s, baseNorm: clamp01(a.norm), seekGen: s.seekGen + 1 } : s;
 52 |     case 'END':
 53 |       // Reached the end: stop and rewind to the start.
 54 |       return { ...s, playing: false, baseNorm: 0, seekGen: s.seekGen + 1 };
 55 |     case 'SET_VOLUME':
 56 |       return { ...s, volume: clamp01(a.value) };
 57 |     case 'SET_EQ':
 58 |       return { ...s, [a.band]: a.value };
 59 |     case 'SET_FILTER':
 60 |       return { ...s, filterCutoff: Math.max(-1, Math.min(1, a.value)) };
 61 |     case 'SET_TEMPO':
 62 |       return { ...s, tempo: clampTempo(a.value) };
 63 |     case 'SET_CUE':
 64 |       return s.track ? { ...s, cuePointNorm: clamp01(a.norm) } : s;
 65 |     case 'GO_TO_CUE':
 66 |       return s.cuePointNorm !== null
 67 |         ? { ...s, baseNorm: s.cuePointNorm, seekGen: s.seekGen + 1 }
 68 |         : s;
 69 |     case 'CLEAR_CUE':
 70 |       return { ...s, cuePointNorm: null };
 71 |     case 'SET_LOOP_IN':
 72 |       return s.track ? { ...s, loopInNorm: clamp01(a.norm) } : s;
 73 |     case 'SET_LOOP_OUT':
 74 |       return s.track ? { ...s, loopOutNorm: clamp01(a.norm) } : s;
 75 |     case 'TOGGLE_LOOP':
 76 |       if (!s.track) return s;
 77 |       // When toggling off, update baseNorm to current position to continue from there
 78 |       if (s.loopEnabled) {
 79 |         // Note: We can't access the current audio position here, so we keep baseNorm as is
 80 |         // The position will naturally continue from where it was
 81 |         return { ...s, loopEnabled: false };
 82 |       }
 83 |       // Only enable if loop points are valid
 84 |       return s.loopInNorm < s.loopOutNorm
 85 |         ? { ...s, loopEnabled: true }
 86 |         : s;
 87 |     case 'CLEAR_LOOP':
 88 |       return { ...s, loopEnabled: false, loopInNorm: 0, loopOutNorm: 1 };
 89 |     default:
 90 |       return s;
 91 |   }
 92 | }
 93 | 
 94 | export interface UseDeck {
 95 |   state: DeckState;
 96 |   position: number; // live normalized playhead 0..1
 97 |   level: number; // live meter level 0..1
 98 |   load: (file: File) => Promise<void>;
 99 |   togglePlay: () => void;
100 |   seek: (norm: number) => void;
101 |   setVolume: (value: number) => void;
102 |   setEq: (band: EqBand, value: number) => void;
103 |   setFilter: (value: number) => void;
104 |   setTempo: (value: number) => void;
105 |   setCue: () => void;
106 |   goToCue: () => void;
107 |   clearCue: () => void;
108 |   setLoopIn: () => void;
109 |   setLoopOut: () => void;
110 |   toggleLoop: () => void;
111 |   clearLoop: () => void;
112 | }
113 | 
114 | export function useDeck(id: string, audioReady: boolean): UseDeck {
115 |   const [state, dispatch] = useReducer(reducer, id, initialDeckState);
116 |   const [position, setPosition] = useState(0);
117 |   const [level, setLevel] = useState(0);
118 | 
119 |   // Ref so the snapshot handler reads current `playing` without re-subscribing.
120 |   const playingRef = useRef(state.playing);
121 |   playingRef.current = state.playing;
122 | 
123 |   // Route this deck's analysis events (playhead + meter) into local state.
124 |   useEffect(() => {
125 |     if (!audioReady) return;
126 |     const rt = getRuntime();
127 |     if (!rt) return;
128 | 
129 |     const posSource = `${id}${POS_EVENT_SUFFIX}`;
130 |     const meterSource = `${id}${METER_EVENT_SUFFIX}`;
131 | 
132 |     const onSnapshot = (e: { source?: string; data: number }) => {
133 |       if (e.source !== posSource) return;
134 |       const p = clamp01(e.data);
135 |       setPosition(p);
136 |       if (p >= 0.9999 && playingRef.current) dispatch({ type: 'END' });
137 |     };
138 | 
139 |     const onMeter = (e: { source?: string; min: number; max: number }) => {
140 |       if (e.source !== meterSource) return;
141 |       setLevel(clamp01(Math.max(Math.abs(e.min), Math.abs(e.max))));
142 |     };
143 | 
144 |     rt.core.on('snapshot', onSnapshot);
145 |     rt.core.on('meter', onMeter);
146 |     return () => {
147 |       rt.core.off('snapshot', onSnapshot);
148 |       rt.core.off('meter', onMeter);
149 |     };
150 |   }, [id, audioReady]);
151 | 
152 |   const load = useCallback(
153 |     async (file: File) => {
154 |       const rt = getRuntime();
155 |       if (!rt) return;
156 |       const track = await loadTrackToVFS(rt, id, file);
157 |       setPosition(0);
158 |       dispatch({ type: 'LOAD', track });
159 |     },
160 |     [id],
161 |   );
162 | 
163 |   const togglePlay = useCallback(() => {
164 |     dispatch(playingRef.current ? { type: 'PAUSE' } : { type: 'PLAY' });
165 |   }, []);
166 | 
167 |   const seek = useCallback((norm: number) => {
168 |     setPosition(clamp01(norm));
169 |     dispatch({ type: 'SEEK', norm });
170 |   }, []);
171 | 
172 |   const setVolume = useCallback((value: number) => dispatch({ type: 'SET_VOLUME', value }), []);
173 |   const setEq = useCallback((band: EqBand, value: number) => dispatch({ type: 'SET_EQ', band, value }), []);
174 |   const setFilter = useCallback((value: number) => dispatch({ type: 'SET_FILTER', value }), []);
175 |   const setTempo = useCallback((value: number) => dispatch({ type: 'SET_TEMPO', value }), []);
176 |   const setCue = useCallback(() => dispatch({ type: 'SET_CUE', norm: position }), [position]);
177 |   const goToCue = useCallback(() => dispatch({ type: 'GO_TO_CUE' }), []);
178 |   const clearCue = useCallback(() => dispatch({ type: 'CLEAR_CUE' }), []);
179 |   const setLoopIn = useCallback(() => dispatch({ type: 'SET_LOOP_IN', norm: position }), [position]);
180 |   const setLoopOut = useCallback(() => dispatch({ type: 'SET_LOOP_OUT', norm: position }), [position]);
181 |   const toggleLoop = useCallback(() => dispatch({ type: 'TOGGLE_LOOP' }), []);
182 |   const clearLoop = useCallback(() => dispatch({ type: 'CLEAR_LOOP' }), []);
183 | 
184 |   return { state, position, level, load, togglePlay, seek, setVolume, setEq, setFilter, setTempo, setCue, goToCue, clearCue, setLoopIn, setLoopOut, toggleLoop, clearLoop };
185 | }
